app.title: jt-ipam
app.tagline: Modern self-hosted IPAM — integrating DNS / LibreNMS / OPNsense / AI, with phpIPAM migration

login.title: Sign in to jt-ipam
login.failed: Login failed; please check your credentials
login.mfa_prompt: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator

certSource.fetched_updated: Fetched a new version and updated
certSource.fetched_skipped: Source certificate is the same as current, no change
certSource.title: Auto-fetch source
certSource.ssh_key_keep: Paste the SSH private key (OpenSSH/PEM); blank = keep stored
certSource.key_managed: Generated and stored by jt-ipam (reopen this dialog to paste your own key instead)
certSource.auth_hint: Used to log in to the SFTP host. Provide a password OR an SSH private key (key takes precedence). The certificate's own private key is the remote key_path file below — unrelated to this.
certSource.gen_key_hint: jt-ipam generates and stores an SSH key; if a login password is set, it logs in and installs the public key for you.
certSource.key_generated: Key generated and stored — copy the public key below onto the host
certSource.key_installed: Key generated and installed on the host
certSource.pub_key_label: Public key (add manually to the SFTP host's ~/.ssh/authorized_keys)
certSource.pub_key_installed: Public key (auto-installed on the SFTP host's ~/.ssh/authorized_keys)

certHelp.title: Install the distribution agent on a site host
certHelp.intro: Install an agent on each host where certificates should auto-rotate. The agent periodically checks jt-ipam for a newer version and, if found, downloads it, writes it to the right paths, runs a config-test, reloads the service, and rolls back on failure.
certHelp.step1: Create an agent in the Distribution Agents tab and copy its one-time key.
certHelp.step2: After creating the agent, open it (the dialog shown right after, or the View action in the list) to get its ready-to-paste one-line install command — key filled in, root/sudo auto-detected. Run it on the target host as root.
certHelp.step3: After pasting the config, run --dry-run on the host to verify; the timer then distributes automatically (advanced: you can also edit the config's DEPLOY_1_* by hand).
certHelp.step3_hint: See "Config help" for the config format and per-profile paths.
certHelp.step3_gen_a: Fastest: in the Distribution Agents list, click the
certHelp.step3_gen_b: tool in that agent's action column, pick certs + services, then paste the result back.
certHelp.distros: Supports Debian 11/12/13, Ubuntu 22.04/24.04/26.04, RHEL/Rocky/AlmaLinux/CentOS, Fedora, openSUSE/SLES (auto-detects apt/dnf/yum/zypper). Keep JT_IPAM_INSECURE=1 if the server uses a self-signed cert.
certHelp.distros_note: Auto-detects apt / dnf / yum / zypper; keep JT_IPAM_INSECURE=1 if the server cert is self-signed.
certHelp.after_install_gen: Once the agent is installed, come back to this list and click the "Generate config" tool in its action column to quickly build its deployment config (which certificates to push, which service profile to use).
certHelp.service_note: Installed units (systemd): jt-ipam-cert-agent.timer (schedule, daily) + jt-ipam-cert-agent.service (each run). Full logs: journalctl -u jt-ipam-cert-agent.service (add -f to follow). Run once now: systemctl start jt-ipam-cert-agent.service.
certHelp.profiles: Built-in profiles: nginx / apache(httpd) / haproxy / postfix / dovecot / pve / pmg / pbs / zimbra / generic (generic requires custom *_path and reload).
certHelp.uninstall_note: Stops and removes the timer / service, the agent program, config and state; certificate files already deployed to your services are left untouched.
certHelp.requirements: Requirements: the agent is pure bash, depending only on curl + coreutils (no Python / jq / YAML); run as root (or an account with permission to write the cert paths and run the reload commands); the installer sets up a systemd timer (daily by default).
certHelp.autoupdate: Self-update: when a newer agent is available on the server, the agent downloads it, verifies the sha256, atomically replaces itself and re-execs (a failure is logged and never affects that run's deployment). Set AUTO_UPDATE=false in config to disable. The Distribution Agents list shows each agent's current version and flags "update available" when it lags.

certConfigHelp.title: Agent config file
certConfigHelp.intro: The config lives at /etc/jt-ipam-cert-agent/config on each host. Each deployment is a group of DEPLOY_<N>_* settings (one per line). Quick mode sets just the certificate + service (profile); manual mode sets custom file paths.

certGen.title: Generate config
certGen.intro: Pick certificates and services to generate the config content; copy it and paste into that host's /etc/jt-ipam-cert-agent/config.
certGen.no_scope: This agent has no certificates in scope yet (add some to the agent on the Certificates page)
certGen.advanced_hint: Use when you want to choose the file paths yourself; leave reload empty to use the chosen profile's reload command.
certGen.empty: (pick certificates and services and the config will appear here)
certGen.files_title: Service config & where the cert files go
certGen.files_hint: The agent writes the certs to the paths below; paste the matching service config (generated for you) into the service's config file.
certGen.no_config_needed: This service reads fixed paths - no service config change needed.
certGen.scope_hint: Only certificates within this agent's scope are listed; to deploy others, add them to the agent on the Certificates page.

certFiles.title: Certificate info / files
certFiles.intro: Per-version details and file download for this certificate (PEM / DER / PKCS#12; formats with the private key are audited).
certFiles.subject: Subject
certFiles.chain_ok_hint: The chain includes the intermediate(s) and the root CA — strict-validating services like Zimbra / PDM will verify it.
certFiles.chain_rebuildable_hint: A full chain to the root CA can be assembled (the root is in the uploaded files, or pulled from the server's system trust store, e.g. ISRG Root X1). Click "Build full chain" to fix it in place (fingerprint unchanged) so Zimbra / PDM will verify.
certFiles.chain_no_root_hint: The chain is missing the root CA. Fine for ordinary services (nginx etc.) but Zimbra / PDM validate strictly and will fail. Get the root certificate from the issuing CA and re-upload it together with the intermediate(s) into the chain field.
certFiles.rebuild_done: Full chain built (fingerprint unchanged; applied on next distribution)

certs.agent_help: Install a distribution agent on each site host (curl installer). The agent periodically checks for a newer certificate version and, if found, downloads it, swaps it in and reloads the service (nginx/apache/pve/pmg/zimbra, etc.). Copy the one-time key shown when you create an agent into the installer.
certs.need_cert_key: Select a certificate file and a private key file
certs.uploaded: New version uploaded and set as current
certs.self_signed_done: Self-signed certificate generated and set as current
certs.self_signed_blocked: Source set or version exists - self-signed disabled (avoids overwriting)
certs.renew_done: Renewed (new version issued and set as current)
certs.renew_hint: Re-issue a new self-signed certificate reusing the current CN and SANs, set as the current version (distribution agents auto-update each site on the next fingerprint change). Adjust the validity days if needed.
certs.multi_ip_hint: Multiple hosts recently reported with the same key ({ips}). Use one key per host, otherwise source IP / version / report status overwrite each other.
certs.one_key_per_host: Use one key per host. Sharing a key across hosts works, but the source IP / version / report status shown here only reflect the most recent host; if recent reports from different IPs share one key, the list flags a warning next to the source IP.
certs.deployed_hint: Successful deployments / total reported (e.g. 0 / 1 = 1 reported, 0 succeeded)
certs.rotate_key: Rotate agent connection key (agent auth, not the SSL cert)
certs.key_once: This is the agent's one-time key — copy it now; it cannot be retrieved later
certs.key_saved: Keep this agent's key safe (you can retrieve it again via "View" in the list)
certs.view_key: View agent connection key & install command
certs.scope_hint: Pick the certificates this agent distributes (empty = none)
certs.device_hint: Pick the device this agent runs on
certs.device_help: Once linked, the agent name in the list and status page links to that device's detail; the source IP also links to its address if it exists in IPAM.

common.confirm_delete: Confirm delete? This action cannot be undone.
common.name: Name
common.confirm_delete_n_irreversible: Delete {n} selected? This action cannot be undone.

scan_agent.outdated_hint: Agent is older than the server (v{v}); it should self-update on the next report. If it stays behind, check that the agent host can overwrite its own program.
scan_agent.deps_hint: Install status of the external tools each probe needs on this scan-agent host.
scan_agent.deps_note: Probes whose tool is missing won't run. On Debian/Ubuntu install with the command above (the agent unlocks it on its next report); on other distros install the equivalent package (e.g. RHEL: samba-common-tools).
scan_agent.scan_now_done: Queued; the agent will run a scan within {n}s.
scan_agent.scan_now_confirm: Run a scan now on this agent's scan-enabled subnets (with their enabled probes)?

os_precedence.title: OS source precedence
os_precedence.hint: When multiple sources (scan agent nmap / LibreNMS / Wazuh) report an OS, take the first one with a value in this order.

scan_probes.title: Scan probes
scan_probes.agent_probes_hint: Choose which probes this agent is allowed to run; default is ICMP only.
scan_probes.excluded_hint: Choose probes to skip for this IP (subtracted from the subnet setting).
scan_probes.effective_hint: Subnet probes - this IP's skips, intersected with the agent's capability.
scan_probes.intrusive_warn: Intrusive probe; IDS may alert. Use a long interval.
scan_probes.agent_unsupported: not supported by this scan agent
scan_probes.install_help_intro: Install the matching tool on the scan-agent host; the probe unlocks on the next check-in:

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.indicator_source: Source: scan agents + LibreNMS + OPNsense ARP (combined)
dashboard.no_subnet_data: No subnet data; create a Section + Subnet and allocate IPs first.

ssh.no_store_hint: Username, password and private key are used only for this session and are never stored on the server.
ssh.store_hint: When checked, this credential is stored encrypted on the server (only you can use it; plaintext is never returned) so you can reuse it next time.
ssh.use_saved_hint: Using a saved credential: the secret is stored encrypted on the server and only decrypted at connect time on the backend; it's never returned to the browser.
ssh.hostkey_body: First connection to {ip}. Please verify the host key fingerprint:
ssh.hostkey_hint: Once trusted it will be pinned; you'll be warned if it changes later (MITM protection).
ssh.enable_hint: When enabled, authorized users can open an SSH terminal from this IP's detail page.
ssh.err_ticket: Cannot establish connection (permission or network issue)

rdp.no_store_hint: Username and password are used only for this session and are not stored on the server.
rdp.store_hint: When checked, this credential is stored encrypted on the server (only you can use it; plaintext is never returned); reusable next time.
rdp.use_saved_hint: Using a saved credential: the password is kept encrypted on the server, decrypted only at connect time on the backend, and never returned to the browser.
rdp.beta_hint: RDP is a beta feature; some cases (special keys, clipboard, remote cursor, concurrency) are still being hardened.
rdp.enable_hint: When enabled, authorized users can open an RDP screen from this IP's detail page (requires the RDP optional dependency installed on the server). This feature is beta.
rdp.err_ticket: Could not establish connection (permission or network issue)
rdp.paste_hint: Send your local clipboard text to the remote, then press Ctrl+V there
rdp.paste_sent: Sent {n} characters to the remote clipboard — press Ctrl+V on the remote to paste
rdp.paste_denied: Cannot read the browser clipboard (allow clipboard permission)

vnc.beta_hint: VNC is a beta feature; some cases (special keys, wheel, clipboard) are still being hardened.
vnc.auth_note: Standard VNC password auth (VncAuth) only — account-based auth such as UltraVNC MS-Logon is not supported.
vnc.no_store_hint: The password is used only for this session and is not stored on the server.
vnc.store_hint: When checked, the password is stored encrypted on the server (only you can use it; plaintext is never returned); reusable next time.
vnc.use_saved_hint: Using a saved password: kept encrypted on the server, decrypted only at connect time on the backend, never returned to the browser.
vnc.enable_hint: When enabled, authorized users can open a VNC screen from this IP's detail page (requires the VNC optional dependency on the server). This feature is beta.
vnc.err_ticket: Could not establish connection (permission or network issue)

connections.hint: Targets with SSH management enabled that you're allowed to connect to. Click 'SSH' for a new tab, or use the dropdown to open in a new window.

users.title: Users
users.can_ssh_hint: When granted, this user can open a terminal to any SSH-enabled IP they can view (no admin or write permission required).
users.error_password_too_short: Password must be at least 12 characters
users.error_password_weak: Password too weak: mix upper/lower case, digits and symbols
users.password_hint: Minimum 12 chars; mix upper/lower case, digits and symbols

ipChanges.title: IP Change Log
ipChanges.subtitle: Change history for all IP addresses (hostname / MAC / ARP / online-offline / manual edits)
ipChanges.search_placeholder: Search IP / field / old·new value / note

scanAgentHelp.title: Scan agent install guide
scanAgentHelp.intro: A scan agent runs on a Linux host inside the target segment and connects OUT to jt-ipam. It periodically pulls the subnets to scan, runs ping/ARP, and pushes results back with its key. The server never needs inbound access to the agent.
scanAgentHelp.step1: Click Create here to make an agent — you get a one-time enrollment key.
scanAgentHelp.step2: On a Linux host in the target segment, run the one-liner below (downloads the agent, sets up systemd, starts reporting).
scanAgentHelp.step3: In Subnets, set the subnets you want scanned to use this agent and enable scanning.
scanAgentHelp.oneliner_label: One-line install (run as root on the agent host)
scanAgentHelp.note: The key is shown only once (at create / rotate). The command includes JT_IPAM_INSECURE=1 for self-signed server certs.
scanAgentHelp.key_title: Agent created — copy the key now
scanAgentHelp.key_warn: The enrollment key for "{name}" is shown only once.
scanAgentHelp.key_note: Paste the one-liner on the agent host; then assign subnets to scan.
scanAgentHelp.install_paths: Installs to: program /opt/jt-ipam-agent/jt_ipam_agent.py, config /etc/jt-ipam-agent.env, service jt-ipam-scan-agent (systemctl status/restart jt-ipam-scan-agent; logs journalctl -u jt-ipam-scan-agent). Uses system python3, no extra packages.
scanAgentHelp.path_python: Uses the system python3 — no extra packages. After a key reset, just re-run the installer (it auto-restarts).
scanAgentHelp.assign_subnets_ph: Pick subnets this agent scans (same setting as the subnet edit page)

chat.model_tip:
Model: {model}
Local inference, nothing leaves the host
chat.model_tip_none: Local inference, nothing leaves the host
chat.placeholder: e.g. list usage of 192.168.1.0/24; find a free IP in 10.0.0.0/24
chat.footer: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline · stays local, never sent externally
chat.confirm_intro: This action changes data — please confirm before it runs:
chat.err_llm: AI assistant unavailable: open Admin → LLM / AI and make sure Ollama is enabled and correctly configured (server URL & model), and that the Ollama server is reachable.
chat.err_generic: Something went wrong with the AI chat, please try again later.
chat.foot_local: AI responses may be wrong — please verify

perm.title: Permissions
perm.pick_principal_first: Pick a user or role first
perm.cascade_hint: Hierarchical: granting a Customer covers its sections/subnets/IPs; granting a Location covers its racks/devices. IPs are huge, so only "All" or inheritance from above.
perm.users_hint: Click a user to assign roles / edit permissions
perm.superuser_note: This user is a superuser and has all permissions (roles don't restrict).
perm.advanced_hint: Grant specific objects directly to this user; normally roles above are enough.

chat_admin.title: Title
chat_admin.purge_confirm: Delete conversations older than the retention period?
chat_admin.retention_hint: 0 = keep forever. Old conversations beyond the retention period are purged on startup; you can also purge manually.

update.banner: An update is available — click here to reload the latest version
update.new_version: A new version v{v} is available —

topology.toggle_hint: Click a legend item to hide / show that node type

hostnameSrc.pin_hint: When pinned, this IP always uses that source's hostname; "Auto" resolves by the global priority order.
hostnameSrc.page_subtitle: When multiple sources (manual / DNS / LibreNMS / OPNsense / scanner / Proxmox VE / Wazuh / AdGuard) report a hostname for the same IP, the first one with a value (top-down) wins. Drag to reorder.
hostnameSrc.drag_hint: Drag to reorder priority (top = highest); use the switch to disable a source — disabled sources are excluded from name matching.
hostnameSrc.arp_subtitle: When multiple sources (scan / OPNsense / LibreNMS / AdGuard / Proxmox VE) report a MAC for the same IP, higher-ranked sources override lower ones; the switch on the right disables a source. Drag to reorder.
hostnameSrc.dev_subtitle: When multiple sources (LibreNMS / DNS / Proxmox VE name / OPNsense / LibreNMS sysName) provide a name for the same device, higher-ranked sources win; Manual is highest and cannot be disabled. Drag to reorder.
hostnameSrc.model_subtitle: When multiple sources (LibreNMS hardware / Proxmox VE / OPNsense) provide a device model, higher = preferred; manual is highest and can't be disabled. Drag to reorder.

addresses.search_placeholder: Search all fields (IP / hostname / MAC / description / owner / switch location / note)
addresses.dhcp_pool_hint: This IP falls within a DHCP server's dynamic lease pool.
addresses.dhcp_has_lease: This IP currently has a DHCP lease (synced from OPNsense).
addresses.switch_port_uncertain: Low confidence: learned via an uplink/trunk (multiple MACs on that port), not a direct access port. LibreNMS did not flag it confident.
addresses.exact_match_hint: When on, IP / hostname must equal the query exactly; off is substring match (e.g. searching 192.168.1.1 no longer also returns 192.168.1.10 / 192.168.1.100). Enter a search term first.

subnets.title: Subnets
subnets.archive_confirm: Archive this subnet? It will be hidden and not scanned, but data is preserved and can be restored from the archive.
subnets.scan_agent_ph: Scan agent (specify for cross-subnet; blank = scan from jt-ipam host directly)
subnets.allow_overlap_hint: Allow overlapping an existing subnet (e.g. same CIDR but different unit / location)

csv_import.title: Import addresses (CSV)
csv_import.hint_html: Column order doesn't matter; header must include ip. Optional: hostname, mac, state, description, owner, switch_port, note. UTF-8 (with BOM); auto-detects comma / semicolon / Tab.
csv_import.dry_run: Dry-run (don't write DB, just preview + errors)
csv_import.queued: Import queued as a background job — see the Tasks page for progress

visualisation.ipv6_pending: IPv6 / non-standard CIDR visualization is coming in Phase 2
visualisation.empty: No registered addresses in this subnet yet
visualisation.agg_hint: Aggregated view for large subnets: each block is a /24, only blocks containing addresses are shown (with used count and usage %)

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.queued_toast: {kind} queued (target: {target}). Check the Tasks page for progress.

devices.link_ip_hint: Pick an existing IP address to assign it to this device (a device can have multiple IPs).
devices.error_location_for_rack: When picking a rack, also pick its location
devices.pick_u_hint: Click a free U row to set the starting U (grey = occupied)

locations.map_all_hint: All locations with coordinates; click a marker to edit.

racks.pin_room: Pin this room (default view on entry)
racks.manage_rooms_hint: A room is a Location; go to the Locations page to add/edit rooms and upload floor-plan backgrounds
racks.room_empty: No racks in this room yet.
racks.fp_remove_confirm: Remove this floor plan? Rack positions will be cleared.
racks.fp_edit_hint: Drag racks to position; click × to send back to tray; Save when done.
racks.fp_view_hint: Click a rack to view its U layout.
racks.diagram_empty: Select a rack to view its U layout.
racks.fp_location_hint: For full rack drag-and-drop placement, open this room in the Racks page.
racks.pick_device_here: Click to place a device in this U
racks.place_device_hint: Places the device into this rack starting at this U position.
racks.seq_ph: Lower number sorts leftmost; blank goes last

requests.title: IP Requests
requests.pick_subnet: Pick a subnet to request from
requests.requested_ip_optional: Requested IP (optional; blank = system allocates first free)

export.label: Export
export.pdf_hint: Print window opened — choose "Save as PDF"
export.popup_blocked: Popup was blocked; allow popups and retry

tools_page.title: Utilities
tools_page.oui_alert_post: file (updating once a month is enough).
tools_page.oui_alert_note: The vendor is shown automatically next to the MAC in all IP lists / detail pages.
tools_page.subtitle: Calculators & lookups for IP / subnet / MAC / DNS / power — all also available in the AI assistant
tools_page.oui_search_ph: Search by prefix (22 / 00:11) or vendor name

dns_admin.scope_hint: For overlapping ranges: only match IPs within these subnets. Empty = global.
dns_admin.title: DNS Servers
dns_admin.help_powerdns: Enter the PowerDNS Authoritative HTTP API URL and X-API-Key (webserver/api must be enabled).
dns_admin.help_bind9: Operate BIND 9 via rndc / TSIG; TSIG key format algorithm:keyname:base64key.
dns_admin.help_unbound_opnsense: Uses the OPNsense Unbound REST API: create an API key/secret in OPNsense with Unbound access.
dns_admin.help_windows_dns: Connects to a Windows DNS host over WinRM; the account needs DNS admin rights — prefer HTTPS (5986).
dns_admin.help_ucs: Calls the Univention UCS UDM REST API (/univention/udm/…) directly — not the internal BIND9. API URL = https://<ucs-host>; use an account with DNS write rights (e.g. Administrator); turn off TLS verification for self-signed certs.

scope_overlap.warning: Overlapping subnets detected (the same IP exists in more than one subnet). This integration has no subnet scope set, so a sync may stamp liveness / DHCP / MAC onto the wrong tenant's copy of an IP. Consider setting the subnet scope below.

librenms_admin.title: LibreNMS
librenms_admin.scope_hint: For overlapping ranges: only match IPs within these subnets. Empty = global.
librenms_admin.auto_create_ips_hint: When on, sync auto-creates an IPAddress for each monitored device's primary IP inside the matching existing subnet (tagged source LibreNMS). Device primary IPs only — not ARP neighbours; if a scope is set, only within that scope. Skipped if the subnet does not exist yet.
librenms_admin.error_token_too_short: API token must be at least 8 chars

firewall.scope_hint: When set, this firewall's NAT rules only resolve to IPs in the scoped subnets; leave empty to use global matching.

firewall_admin.title: OPNsense Firewall
firewall_admin.name: Name
firewall_admin.tls_skip_warning: TLS verification disabled — traffic can be intercepted by MITM. Only use on trusted internal networks.
firewall_admin.tls_verified_hint: Recommended: validate the OPNsense certificate to prevent MITM.
firewall_admin.tls_alert_body: {n} firewall(s) skip TLS verification: {names}. Use a trusted certificate and enable verification.
firewall_admin.version_hint: OPNsense 24.1+ recommended. Basic sync (DHCP/ARP/rules/NAT) works on most versions; VPN sync needs the os-wireguard plugin (WireGuard) and the newer swanctl connections API for IPsec (~24.x+). Use a read-only API account.
firewall_admin.mappings_hint: This tab manages jt-ipam → OPNsense alias mappings (push IPAM objects, e.g. a subnet's IPs, into an OPNsense alias). Click "Create mapping"; it's empty until you add one.
firewall_admin.rules_hint: Only rules created via OPNsense's "Automation → Filter" plugin can be synced; classic "Firewall → Rules" are not exposed by the OPNsense API, so they can't appear here (empty is normal). OPNsense 26.1+ provides a tool to migrate legacy rules into automation filter rules, which can then be synced.
firewall_admin.pick_firewall_to_view: Pick a firewall to view rules
firewall_admin.sync_interval_hint: The systemd timer (jt-ipam-sync.timer) checks every minute; instances past this interval auto-sync. Manual "Sync" is not limited.
firewall_admin.sync_sources_hint: When on, sync pulls matching OPNsense data, stamps matched IPs’ last-seen (scanner), and auto-fills MAC / hostname.
firewall_admin.aliases_hint: Alias definitions pulled from OPNsense (read-only); refreshed when you sync the firewall.
firewall_admin.expose_dsv_label: Expose firewall DSV (alias lookup for Graylog)

wazuh_admin.scope_hint: For overlapping ranges: only match IPs within these subnets. Empty = global.
wazuh_admin.title: Wazuh
wazuh_admin.api_user_ph: wazuh-wui or a custom API user

custom_fields.name: Field key (internal)
custom_fields.name_hint: Lowercase letters/digits/underscore; must start with a letter; max 64 chars; immutable
custom_fields.options_placeholder:
One per line, e.g.
production
staging
dev
custom_fields.regex_hint: Input must match; e.g. plate number ^[A-Z]{3}-[0-9]{4}$
custom_fields.error_name_format: Invalid name: lowercase letters/digits/underscore; letter-start; max 64 chars
custom_fields.error_options_required: select / multi_select needs at least one choice

nat.alias_goto: Click to view this alias on the Firewall page
nat.confirm_delete_n: Delete {n} selected NAT rules? This action cannot be undone.

anomaly.title: Anomaly Detection
anomaly.help: Click the button to run all detection rules: IP conflicts, MAC drift, ghost IPs, unauthorized IPs.

webhooks.events_hint: Tick the events to subscribe; tick "*" to subscribe to all (including events added later).
webhooks.ev_all: All events (current and any added in the future)
webhooks.ev_ipreq_created: When someone submits an IP request
webhooks.ev_ipreq_fulfilled: When an IP request is approved and an address is allocated
webhooks.ev_ipreq_rejected: When an IP request is rejected
webhooks.ev_anomaly: When an anomaly is detected (e.g. MAC drift, a new device)
webhooks.secret_warning: The secret below is shown only once — copy it now and store it on your end to verify the X-jt-ipam-Signature header.

advanced.error_circuit_required: CID, Provider, and Type are required

circuits.type_placeholder: Pick one, or type a new type and press Enter to add
circuits.types_hint: Manage the selectable circuit types here; new types appear in the “Type” dropdown of the circuit form above. Deleting a type does not delete circuits — it only clears their type to uncategorised.
circuits.device_ph: Pick the device this circuit terminates on

virt.scope_hint: For overlapping ranges: only match IPs within these subnets. Empty = global.
virt.token_hint: Create an API Token under Datacenter → Permissions → API Tokens, and grant read-only privileges.
virt.help_title: How to get an API Token
virt.help_step1: In Proxmox VE, create a Token under Datacenter → Permissions → API Tokens (tip: uncheck "Privilege Separation" for simplicity).
virt.help_step2: Under Datacenter → Permissions → Add → API Token Permission, assign the token as:
virt.help_step3: Fill the Token ID and Secret above back into this form.
virt.err_need_cluster: Please select a cluster first (create one in the Clusters tab if none exist)
virt.err_token_secret: Token Secret must be at least 8 characters
virt.multinode_hint: You can add multiple node connections per cluster. On sync the system auto-selects a healthy node to pull the whole cluster, failing over to another node if one is down.
virt.extra_urls_ph: One per line, e.g. https://pve2.example.com:8006; used when the primary is unreachable
virt.cluster_auto_ph: Leave blank: auto-created from the PVE cluster name on sync

physical.pick_both_ports: Select a port on both ends
physical.same_port: Both ends can't be the same port

migration.title: phpIPAM migration
migration.help: Pull from your existing phpIPAM MySQL. First run dry-run to preview, then commit. on_conflict=skip is safe to re-run repeatedly during parallel use (already-imported rows untouched).
migration.intro_combined: Pull from phpIPAM. Private key is used once and not stored. Use dry-run first.
migration.via_socket_hint: SSH-forwards local TCP to the phpIPAM host's Unix socket — MySQL's auth_socket plugin authenticates by SSH OS user, so no DB credentials needed. SSH user must have read access to phpIPAM DB (root usually does).
migration.via_tcp_hint: TCP-connect to 127.0.0.1:3306 on the phpIPAM host — requires MySQL username / password.
migration.username_socket_hint: auth_socket plugin matches OS peer credential to MySQL user; leaving empty uses the SSH OS user.
migration.persist_hint: Stored in localStorage (this browser only). Private key and MySQL password are never saved.
migration.key_stored: Private key stored (encrypted); reusable across browsers. Leave blank to reuse it.
migration.persist_cleared: Saved settings cleared on this browser
migration.use_ssh_hint: phpIPAM MySQL usually only listens on 127.0.0.1; tunnel is typically required
migration.ssh_help: Private key is used only in this request and is not stored in jt-ipam. SSH connection closes after the import. Recommended: add a temporary key on the phpIPAM host and remove it after migration.
migration.ssh_private_key_placeholder:
-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
b3BlbnNzaC1rZXktdjEAAAAA...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
migration.ssh_known_host_placeholder: Click 'Fetch host fingerprint' to populate; or paste single-line ssh-keyscan output manually
migration.ssh_keypair_title: Steps (do this once for the first phpIPAM import)
migration.ssh_step1_html: On any machine (jt-ipam server itself is fine) run: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f phpipam_migrate -N '' — produces phpipam_migrate (private) and phpipam_migrate.pub (public).
migration.ssh_step2_html: Append phpipam_migrate.pub to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the phpIPAM host (for the SSH user below). e.g. cat phpipam_migrate.pub | ssh root@phpipam-host 'cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys'
migration.ssh_step3_html: Back here, paste the private key (entire phpipam_migrate file) into the field below → click 'Fetch host fingerprint' → run import.
migration.ssh_step4_html: After import, remove that key from the phpIPAM host's authorized_keys. The private key is not stored in jt-ipam — used once per request.
migration.ssh_subsection_key: 2. SSH private key (matching the public key from step 2)
migration.ssh_host_hint: Hostname or IP of the machine running phpIPAM.
migration.ssh_user_hint: Must be able to SSH in and read phpIPAM DB — usually root or the phpipam owner.
migration.ssh_private_key_hint: Paste the private key that can log into the phpIPAM host. Used once, not stored.
migration.ssh_known_host_hint: Used to verify the host is who it claims to be (anti-MITM).
migration.known_host_set: Host public key set; ready to import
migration.tofu_warn: First time connecting to this SSH host. Confirm the fingerprint below via an out-of-band channel (phone / host console) to avoid MITM.
migration.tofu_compare: On the phpIPAM host, run this to verify:
migration.error_ssh_required: ssh_host and ssh_user are required when using SSH tunnel
migration.error_known_host_required: Click 'Fetch host fingerprint' to confirm the host first
migration.result_partial_failure_body: {n} row(s) failed — see errors below and retry.
migration.queued_waiting: Queued as a background task, waiting for result…
migration.host_key_changed: SSH host key changed! expected={expected}, actual={actual}
migration.task_running_body: Import running in background ({status} · {progress}%)… results show automatically when done; you can also watch live progress on the Tasks page.

import.title: RIPE / TWNIC import
import.ripe_help: Pull inetnum data for the given handle or CIDR from RIPE WHOIS. Preview shows what would be imported; commit writes it in.
import.either_or_hint: Fill in either Handle or CIDR (not both).
import.error_need_one: Provide at least a Handle or a CIDR
import.error_pick_one: Provide only one of Handle or CIDR

security.totp_intro_html: Requires a 6-digit code in addition to your password at login. We recommend open-source apps: KeePassXC, Aegis (Android), Raivo OTP (iOS), 2FAS; also works with Google Authenticator / 1Password / Authy.
security.disable_confirm: Disable TOTP? Future logins will no longer require a 6-digit code.
security.step1: Step 1: scan the QR code with an authenticator app
security.step2: Step 2: enter the 6-digit code shown by the app

prefs.online_grace_minutes_hint: An IP is treated as offline if its last_seen is older than this. Default 30 minutes; set ≥ 2x your scan interval to avoid flapping.

system.map_provider_hint: Global setting: the Locations map preview. Default "Built-in" is fully offline with no external calls; or use OpenStreetMap (tiles proxied same-origin, browser never hits OSM directly) or Google Maps (in-page uses the built-in map; the external link opens Google).
system.geoip_hint: Enter your MaxMind Account ID and License Key (stored encrypted). When a local mmdb is downloaded, lookups use it (offline, fast); otherwise they fall back to the GeoLite2 web service.
system.geoip_need_creds: Set and save MaxMind credentials first
system.geoip_freq_advice: Update frequency: GeoLite2 (free) is published Tue & Fri → 'Twice weekly' is ideal (daily is pointless). GeoIP2 (paid) City/Country is published weekly → 'Weekly' suffices; choose Daily only if you need the freshest.
system.geoip_asn_note: Note: GeoLite2-ASN is free, but some older / not-enabled MaxMind free accounts may not be entitled to it. If the ASN download fails, check your MaxMind account is subscribed to GeoLite2 ASN.
system.rack_name_align_hint: Horizontal alignment of device names in the rack diagram.
system.ldap_enable: Enable LDAP / AD auth (pick the "LDAP / AD" realm on the login page; account auto-created on first successful login)
system.ldap_user_filter_hint: AD: (sAMAccountName={username}); OpenLDAP often (uid={username}). {username} is substituted with the login name.
system.ldap_admin_groups_hint: Users in these groups get admin rights automatically on login.
system.ldap_default_role_hint: Non-admin LDAP accounts auto-created on first login join this role (group) for an initial visibility scope; leave empty to require manual assignment.
system.ldap_hint: Settings are stored in the database with the bind password encrypted (AES-GCM); changes apply immediately, no restart. Users log in with their AD credentials in the normal login form.
system.oidc_admin_groups_ph: Comma-separated; members become admins on login
system.oidc_hint: Stored in the database with the client secret encrypted (AES-GCM); changes apply immediately. The login page shows "Sign in with OIDC". The redirect URI must match what's registered at the IdP.
system.saml_idp_metadata_xml_ph: If you have no URL, paste the IdP metadata XML
system.saml_auto_ph: Blank = auto-derive from this site's URL
system.saml_hint: Stored in the database with the SP private key encrypted (AES-GCM); changes apply immediately. The login page shows "Sign in with SAML". Give the IdP the "Download SP metadata" link above to register.
system.ldap_authtest_hint: Runs the real flow (admin bind → find user by filter → bind as that user); on success shows the resolved DN and admin flag, to diagnose filter/credentials.
system.af_title: Forward audit logs to log server
system.af_enable: Forward audit events (sent live on login / data changes)
system.af_hint: Best-effort; failures never affect the app. GELF default port 12201; syslog often 514. GELF over TCP is NULL-delimited, over UDP sends raw JSON (fine for small events; prefer TCP at volume).
system.graylog_enable: Enable the DSV lookup endpoint (for Graylog to poll)
system.graylog_url_http_hint: Served on dedicated port 8088, unencrypted; the token is exposed on the wire — trusted LAN only. To disable, remove the 8088 server block in the nginx config.
system.graylog_hint: In Graylog, add a “DSV File from HTTP” data adapter and paste the URL above; pick separator , or Tab per format, key column = 1, value column = 2 (key = IP, value = hostname / FQDN). Regenerate the token any time to revoke access.
system.graylog_fw_intro: Besides IP→hostname, each OPNsense firewall with “Expose firewall DSV” enabled gets two lookup tables: ① rule label→alias (use the filterlog rid to find which alias a packet hit) and ② alias→member list. They reuse the same token as above. Enable it per firewall under Integrations → OPNsense.
system.graylog_fw_need_token: No token yet: enable the Graylog DSV above first (it generates a token) so these firewall lookups have working URLs.
system.graylog_fw_none: No firewall has “Expose firewall DSV” enabled. Edit a firewall under Integrations → OPNsense and tick it.
system.graylog_fw_rules_url: Rule DSV (key = filterlog rid, value = alias name)
system.graylog_fw_aliases_url: Alias DSV (key = alias name, value = member list)
system.graylog_fw_hint: On the Graylog side, add a separate “DSV File from HTTP” adapter + lookup table for each: bind the rule DSV lookup to the filterlog rid field, key column = 0, value column = 1 (Graylog column indices are 0-based). Each firewall has its own URL (with its firewall id).
system.graylog_need_token: No token yet; the lookup endpoint URLs below need one.
system.graylog_tbl_hint: Click a row to show its setup guide below; click “URLs / settings” for the full URLs (HTTPS / intranet HTTP) and per-source settings (the IP→hostname enable/path live there too). Firewall DSVs are enabled per firewall under Integrations → OPNsense (“Expose firewall DSV”).
system.graylog_pve_hint: key = Proxmox VMID, value = VM name (from synced Proxmox VE virtual machines). On the Graylog side add a “DSV File from HTTP” adapter + lookup table to turn a log's vmid field into the VM name. If vmids collide across clusters, only the first per vmid is emitted.
system.graylog_page_intro: jt-ipam generates a live IP → hostname / FQDN lookup endpoint so Graylog can automatically enrich log events that only carry an IP. Enable the endpoint and grab the URL here, then wire it into Graylog using the guide below.
system.graylog_guide_card: How to wire it into Graylog
system.graylog_g_how: How it works: Graylog uses a Lookup Table that periodically polls the DSV URL above, looks up the IP field of each message and writes the hostname into a new field. The table only contains IPs that have a hostname.
system.graylog_g_click_copy: Tip: the highlighted values below are what you paste into Graylog — click any of them to copy.
system.graylog_g_lt_intro: A Graylog lookup is made of three pieces, created in order under System → Lookup Tables: a Data Adapter (the source), a Cache, and the Lookup Table (which binds the two). The Title / Description / Name in each table below can be used as-is.
system.graylog_g_adapter_desc: IP → hostname mapping (served by the jt-ipam DSV endpoint)
system.graylog_g_url_or_http: or plain HTTP (dedicated port 8088, trusted LAN only):
system.graylog_g_ipfield_label: Your IP field name (auto-filled into the sample below):
system.graylog_g_field_err_chars: Graylog field names may only contain letters, digits and underscores (_)
system.graylog_g_field_err_digit: Graylog field names cannot start with a digit
system.graylog_g_cache_desc: Cache for the jt-ipam IP lookup table
system.graylog_g_lt_desc: Resolve a source IP to its hostname / FQDN
system.graylog_g_pick_adapter: Select the Data Adapter created above
system.graylog_g_leave_empty: Leave empty (no value when not found)
system.graylog_g_apply_title: Apply to logs (Extractor or Pipeline — pick one)
system.graylog_g_apply_intro: Pick just one of the two methods below — you don't need both: an Extractor is the simplest and best for a single Input with simple rules; a Pipeline is more flexible and can span multiple Streams / add conditions.
system.graylog_g_ex: Go to System → Inputs, pick your Input → “Manage extractors” → “Get started”, choose a message that has the field → add a “Lookup Table” extractor on the source field → pick the lookup table you created and store the result in a new field. Every incoming message then gets enriched automatically. Best for a single Input with simple rules.
system.graylog_g_pl_title: Apply the lookup with a Pipeline
system.graylog_g_pl: For multiple streams or conditional logic, use a Pipeline instead. Under System → Pipelines create a pipeline connected to the target Stream, and in a stage add a rule that calls lookup_value() to query the table and set_field() to write the result. The first argument of lookup_value is the Lookup Table name (not the path).
system.graylog_g_pl_fw: Under System → Pipelines add a rule that calls lookup_value() to fetch this DSV's value and set_field() to write a new field. The first argument of lookup_value is the Lookup Table name (not the path). Set the “log field” (configured in Step 2) to the parsed filterlog field: bind the rule DSV to rid, the alias DSV to your alias field.
system.graylog_g_field_label: Log field to look up (auto-filled into the example below):
system.graylog_g_lan: Looking up public IPs against this table is pointless (it only contains your internal IPs). In the pipeline rule, use cidr_match() to first test whether the IP falls within the RFC1918 private ranges (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) and only look it up when it does — saving resources and avoiding false hits. Replace src_ip with your actual IP field name:
system.graylog_g_note: Tip: the lookup_value() key must be cast with to_string(); the second argument of cidr_match() must be cast with to_ip(). If your messages carry both source and destination IPs, duplicate the rule for dst_ip → dst_hostname.
system.rdp_clip_paste: Allow RDP paste from controller to remote
system.rdp_clip_paste_hint: When enabled, the RDP toolbar shows a Paste button that pushes your local clipboard text into the remote clipboard (one-way, text only — then press Ctrl+V on the remote). Off by default; the remote clipboard is never sent back.
system.change_log_dim_days_hint: Change-log entries older than this many days are shown dimmed so recent changes stand out. 0 = never dim. Default 30.

errors.session_expired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
errors.admin_required: Administrator privileges are required for this action
errors.no_visible_resources: You have no accessible resources yet; please ask an administrator to grant access

adguard_admin.scope_hint: For overlapping ranges: only match IPs within these subnets. Empty = global.

llm_settings.title: LLM / AI global settings
llm_settings.admin_note: This page holds {strong}; changes overwrite .env and persist to system_settings. All users and automation (embedding / chat / MCP) share this config.
llm_settings.num_ctx_hint: Max tokens the chat model can see at once. Blank or 0 = use the model/Ollama default (usually 4096). Tool-heavy chats with large injected data can exceed the default and get truncated (the model 'forgets' earlier content); raise it to e.g. 8192 / 16384 / 32768. Larger uses more RAM/VRAM. Affects chat only, not the embedding model.
llm_settings.mcp_ext_title: Expose MCP (let other systems call it)
llm_settings.mcp_ext_hint: When on, other systems (external LLM clients, automation) can call this site's MCP endpoint over HTTP to query data. Off by default — must be turned on to accept external calls.
llm_settings.mcp_transport_note: (in your client pick HTTP / Streamable, not SSE)
llm_settings.mcp_auth_choose: either one (header name on the left, value on the right):
llm_settings.mcp_key_rotate_confirm: Regenerating immediately invalidates the old key; connected systems must update. Continue?
llm_settings.mcp_key_hint: This key is read-only: external systems can only query — the 6 data-changing tools are always blocked. It is stored encrypted and authorized within the current admin's visibility; regenerate immediately if leaked.
llm_settings.mcp_gen_hint: Paste the snippet into the tool's config file to connect to this MCP. The API key is filled in for you; exact schema may vary by tool/version.
llm_settings.mcp_gen_inline_hint: One-click config for Claude Desktop / opencode / mcpo and more
llm_settings.tools_intro: The AI assistant and external MCP clients can call these {n} tools ({m} of which change data and require confirmation). Read-only tools answer instantly; mutating ones pause in the AI chat for you to confirm.

rack_diagram.export_svg_hint: Export as editable SVG (import into draw.io to edit text / shapes / lines)

request_detail.approve_confirm: This will allocate an IP and notify the requester. Continue?
request_detail.reject_reason_ph: Explain the reason for rejection (the requester will be notified)
request_detail.title: IP Request
request_detail.target_ip_ph: Blank = system picks the first free IP
request_detail.target_auto_hint: (first free IP picked by the system — you can change it)

subnet_detail.scan_agent_ph: Specify for cross-subnet scanning; blank = scan from the jt-ipam host directly
subnet_detail.delete_confirm: Delete this subnet? This cannot be undone. If it still has IPs or child subnets, clear them first.

version.title: Version Info
version.check_failed: Could not reach GitHub to check
version.packages_hint: Key packages actually installed on the backend.

dns_records.title: DNS Records
dns_records.intro: Only A / AAAA / PTR records (for IP↔name mapping); other types like CNAME, MX, TXT are not included. Filter by server / type, search, look up records by IP, or show only A/AAAA records whose target IP has no matching address in IPAM.

req_policy.title: IP Request Approval
req_policy.intro: Configure who can approve / reject IP requests. When a request is submitted, all approvers get an in-app notification (and an email if the Email channel is configured).
req_policy.mode_admin: System administrators only (single gate, one approval allocates)
req_policy.mode_designated: Administrators + designated users / groups (single gate, any one approves)
req_policy.mode_parallel: Parallel sign-off (multiple gates, any order, all must approve)
req_policy.mode_stages: Sequential gates (must pass gate 1→2→3…; final approval allocates)
req_policy.parallel_note: Sign-off: every gate below must be approved (any order); the IP is allocated once all pass.
req_policy.stages_note: Sequential: gates must pass top to bottom; each gate's approvers are notified only when it's their turn.
req_policy.stage_need_approver: Each gate needs at least one approver or group
req_policy.designated_note: Designated approvers can approve requests for ANY subnet (global approvers).
req_policy.self_approve: Allow approvers to approve their own requests
req_policy.self_approve_hint: Off by default (separation of duties); when off, an approver cannot approve a request they submitted themselves.

notify_ch.title: Notification Channels
notify_ch.intro: Configure outbound notification delivery. Email is supported now; other messengers are in development. In-app bell notifications work without any setup.
notify_ch.password_keep: Set; leave blank to keep unchanged
notify_ch.no_host: Enter the SMTP host first (no need to enable — you can send a test right away).
notify_ch.matrix_hint: Choose which channels each event notifies through.
notify_ch.matrix_email_note: The Email column only sends if the Email (SMTP) channel above is enabled; the in-app bell always works.

ev.cert_drift: Certificate drift (an agent is not up to date)

oui_admin.title: MAC Vendor Database (OUI)
oui_admin.schedule_note: Auto-refreshed monthly on the 1st (systemd timer). You can also refresh manually here.

ports.title: Ports / Cabling
ports.import_no_source: No linked LibreNMS source for this device

pfsense_admin.title: pfSense firewall
pfsense_admin.name_url_required: Name and API URL are required
pfsense_admin.api_hint: pfSense CE 2.8.x has no built-in REST API. Install the pfSense-pkg-RESTAPI package (pfrest.org), then under System → REST API → Settings add "API Key" to the auth methods and create a key under Keys. Base path /api/v2, X-API-Key auth; turn off Verify TLS for a self-signed cert.
pfsense_admin.rules_empty: No rules (enable Rules sync and run once)

pfsense_view.hint: pfSense firewall rules & aliases (read-only; enable rules/alias sync on the pfSense settings page and run it).

novnc.cred_hint: Connecting logs in to the cluster with the PVE credentials you enter; you must have Console permission on this VM/CT (not stored on the server).
novnc.err_ticket: Failed to obtain a connection ticket
novnc.err_generic: Cannot open PVE console (this IP is not a PVE VM/CT, or no permission)
novnc.enable_hint: This IP maps to a Proxmox VE VM/CT; an in-browser console is available.
novnc.no_store_hint: Credentials are used only for this session (not stored); you must have Console permission on this VM/CT.
novnc.use_saved_hint: Using a saved PVE credential: the password is stored encrypted on the server, decrypted only at connect time, never returned to the browser.
